name: Calls
description: Integrates real-time voice communication in Mattermost

settings_schema.header: Calls plugin enables voice calls with screensharing in channels. See [documentation](https://mattermost.com/pl/calls-make?utm_source=mattermost&utm_medium=in-product&utm_content=calls_admin_settings) to learn more.

sections.title: General Settings
sections.subtitle: Settings for participants, screen sharing, ringing, and more

settings.help_text: When test mode is enabled, only system admins are able to start calls in channels. This allows testing to confirm calls are working as expected.
The maximum number of participants that can join a call. If left empty, or set to 0, an unlimited number of participants can join.
When set to true, call participants can share their screen.
When set to true, simulcast for screen sharing is enabled. This can help to improve screen sharing quality.

When set to true, ringing functionality is enabled: participants in direct or group messages will receive a desktop alert and a ringing notification when a call is started. Changing this setting requires a plugin restart.

settings.display_name: Enable AV1 codec for screen sharing (Experimental)
settings.help_text:
When set to true it enables using the AV1 codec to encode screen sharing tracks. This can result in improved screen sharing quality for clients that support it.
Note: this setting won't apply when EnableSimulcast is true.

When set to true, clients will use WebRTC data channels for signaling of new media tracks. This can result in a more efficient and less race-prone process, especially in case of frequent WebSocket disconnections.
When set to true, video calls are enabled in direct message channels.

sections.title: LiveKit Service
sections.subtitle: Configure a LiveKit server for WebRTC media routing

settings.help_text: (Optional) The URL of a LiveKit server instance to use for call media routing (e.g. wss://livekit.example.com).
The API key used to authenticate with the LiveKit server.
The API secret used to authenticate with the LiveKit server.

sections.title: Call recordings
sections.subtitle: Recordings include the entire call window view along with participants’ audio track and any shared screen video. Recordings are stored in Mattermost

settings.help_text: (Optional) When set to true, call recordings are enabled.
The URL to a running calls job service instance used for call recordings.
The maximum duration (in minutes) for call recordings. Value must be in the range [15, 180].

The audio and video quality of call recordings.
 Note: this setting can affect the overall performance of the job service and the number of concurrent recording jobs that can be run.

sections.title: Call transcriptions
sections.subtitle: Allows calls to be transcribed to text files. Recordings must be enabled

settings.help_text: (Optional) When set to true, post-call transcriptions are enabled.
The speech-to-text API to use for post-call transcriptions.
The speech-to-text model size to use for post-call transcriptions. Heavier models will produce more accurate results at the expense of processing time and resources usage.
The number of threads used by the post-call transcriber. This must be in the range [1, numCPUs].
The API key for Azure Speech Services
The API region for Azure Speech Services

sections.title: Live captions
sections.subtitle: Displays spoken words as text captions during a call. Recordings and transcriptions must be enabled

settings.help_text: (Optional) When set to true, live captions are enabled.
The speech-to-text model size to use for live captions. Heavier models will produce more accurate results at the expense of processing time and resources usage.

settings.display_name: Live captions: Number of transcribers used per call
settings.help_text: The number of separate live-captions transcribers for each call. Each transcribes one audio stream at a time. The product of LiveCaptionsNumTranscribers * LiveCaptionsNumThreadsPerTranscriber must be in the range [1, numCPUs].

settings.display_name: Live captions: Number of threads per transcriber
settings.help_text: The number of threads per live-captions transcriber. The product of LiveCaptionsNumTranscribers * LiveCaptionsNumThreadsPerTranscriber must be in the range [1, numCPUs].
The language passed to the live captions transcriber. Should be a 2-letter ISO 639 Set 1 language code, e.g. 'en'. If blank, will be set to English 'en' as default.
When test mode is enabled, only system admins are able to start calls in channels. This allows testing to confirm calls are working as expected.
(Optional) The URL of a LiveKit server instance to use for call media routing (e.g. wss://livekit.example.com).
The API key used to authenticate with the LiveKit server.
The API secret used to authenticate with the LiveKit server.
The maximum number of participants that can join a call. If left empty, or set to 0, an unlimited number of participants can join.
When set to true, call participants can share their screen.
When set to true, simulcast for screen sharing is enabled. This can help to improve screen sharing quality.
(Optional) When set to true, call recordings are enabled.
The URL to a running calls job service instance used for call recordings.
The maximum duration (in minutes) for call recordings. Value must be in the range [15, 180].

The audio and video quality of call recordings.
 Note: this setting can affect the overall performance of the job service and the number of concurrent recording jobs that can be run.
(Optional) When set to true, post-call transcriptions are enabled.
The speech-to-text API to use for post-call transcriptions.
The speech-to-text model size to use for post-call transcriptions. Heavier models will produce more accurate results at the expense of processing time and resources usage.
The API key for Azure Speech Services
The API region for Azure Speech Services
The number of threads used by the post-call transcriber. This must be in the range [1, numCPUs].
(Optional) When set to true, live captions are enabled.
The speech-to-text model size to use for live captions. Heavier models will produce more accurate results at the expense of processing time and resources usage.

settings.help_text: The number of separate live-captions transcribers for each call. Each transcribes one audio stream at a time. The product of LiveCaptionsNumTranscribers * LiveCaptionsNumThreadsPerTranscriber must be in the range [1, numCPUs].

settings.display_name: Live captions: Number of threads per transcriber
settings.help_text: The number of threads per live-captions transcriber. The product of LiveCaptionsNumTranscribers * LiveCaptionsNumThreadsPerTranscriber must be in the range [1, numCPUs].
The language passed to the live captions transcriber. Should be a 2-letter ISO 639 Set 1 language code, e.g. 'en'. If blank, will be set to English 'en' as default.

When set to true, ringing functionality is enabled: participants in direct or group messages will receive a desktop alert and a ringing notification when a call is started. Changing this setting requires a plugin restart.

settings.help_text:
When set to true it enables using the AV1 codec to encode screen sharing tracks. This can result in improved screen sharing quality for clients that support it.
Note: this setting won't apply when EnableSimulcast is true.

When set to true, clients will use WebRTC data channels for signaling of new media tracks. This can result in a more efficient and less race-prone process, especially in case of frequent WebSocket disconnections.
When set to true, video calls are enabled in direct message channels.
